Diff (+2057/-24):
PR DESCRIPTION:
<!-- Thank you for submitting a pull request! Find more information 
at https://trino.io/development/process.html, 
at https://github.com/trinodb/trino/blob/master/.github/DEVELOPMENT.md 
and contact us on #core-dev in Slack. -->
<!-- Provide an overview for maintainers and reviewers. -->
## Description

Removes expired snapshots of MVs on refresh, up to a cap. Previously, this feature was implemented without a cap, but was reverted due to performance implications. The cap should mitigate those, and can be configured.

The two config properties that were added are:
- `iceberg.materialized-views.refresh-max-snapshots-to-expire`, defining the cap
- `iceberg.materialized-views.refresh-snapshot-retention-period`, defining a retention period to avoid removing snapshots used in ongoing queries

A new test was added to verify behavior. As this behavior depends on config properties, I used a pre-made MV to build a backlog of snapshots that should be removed to verify that the cap applies properly.

<!-- Provide details that help an engineer who is unfamiliar with this part of the code. -->
## Additional context and related issues

- First implementation: https://github.com/trinodb/trino/pull/25343
- Revert: https://github.com/trinodb/trino/pull/26051
- Related issue: https://github.com/trinodb/trino/issues/27598

<!-- Mark the appropriate option with an (x). Propose a release note if you can.
More info at https://trino.io/development/process#release-note -->
#

--- docs/src/main/sphinx/connector/iceberg.md (+15/-0) ---
@@ -184,6 +184,21 @@ implementation is used:
     creation of more data files, since it uses the append operation to insert
     the new records.
   - `true`
+* - `iceberg.materialized-views.refresh-max-snapshots-to-expire`
+  - Maximum number of materialized view snapshots to expire when performing a
+    refresh. A value of `0` will result in no snapshots being removed. Higher
+    values may increase the time taken to execute materialized view refreshes
+    when there are a large number of snapshots in the materialized view. Any
+    snapshots within the duration of the
+    `iceberg.materialized-views.refresh-snapshot-retention-period` configuration
+    property will be retained.
+  - `200`
+* - `iceberg.materialized-views.refresh-snapshot-retention-period`
+  - The duration for which materialized view snapshots will be retained. Any
+    snapshots older than this value will be removed during a materialized view
+    refresh, up to a cap defined by the configuration property
+    `iceberg.materialized-views.refresh-max-snapshots-to-expire`.
+  - `4h`
 * - `iceberg.metadata-cache.enabled`
   - Set to `false` to disable in-memory caching of metadata files on the
     coordinator. This cache is not used when `fs.cache.enabled` is set to true.

POINTS:
- [core/trino-main/src/main/java/io/trino/FeaturesConfig.java:137] I don't think it should be an Enum if there are only two possible values.
In the parquet reader also we have an auto detection logic that may decide to skip Vector API even if the flag is enabled, that didn't justify turning it into an Enum
- [core/trino-main/src/main/java/io/trino/FeaturesConfig.java:376] `exchange.experimental.vectorized-serde.enabled`
blockserde...null-suppression implies a very specific thing, we may want to use Vector API for more than just that
having a more generic name keeps the door open to adding more usage under same flag and is easier for users to grasp
- [core/trino-main/src/main/java/io/trino/FeaturesConfig.java:377] Enable using Java Vector API for faster serialization and deserialization of exchange data
- [core/trino-main/src/test/java/io/trino/execution/buffer/BenchmarkBlockSerde.java:84] I don't follow why this is better.
The actual size of pages produced in benchmark setup is decided by `io.trino.spi.PageBuilder#isFull` and that is unchanged. But we have now reduced the total number of rows generated as input for the benchmark, I think this will cut input of the benchmark to one 8K Page. Wouldn't we want to run the JMH for at least a few dozen pages ?
- [core/trino-main/src/main/java/io/trino/simd/BlockEncodingSimdSupport.java:76] Lack of ARM support is a bummer. Graviton machines do so much better in the benchmarks that its heavily favored in production deployments at the moment and an incremental gain from better vectorization on intel wouldn't move the needle in the other direction.
- [core/trino-main/src/main/java/io/trino/simd/BlockEncodingSimdSupport.java:110] It seems overkill to add a separate flag per byte species.
I think we should simplify this to check only for `AVX512VBMI2` support as that is an extension on `AVX512F`, and fall back to scalar if AVX512VBMI2 support is not found.
I would also do the vector bit size in a similar way as `io.trino.parquet.reader.ColumnReaderFactory#getVectorBitSize` to ensure that we don't get failures when the JVM flag for enabling vector preview is not enabled.
- [core/trino-spi/pom.xml:266] Instead of boolean parameter to existing BlockEncoding classes, couldn't you create new Vector...BlockEncoding classes and register them as
```
if (vectorEnabled) {
     addBlockEncoding(new ByteArrayBlockEncoding());
}
else {
     addBlockEncoding(new VectorByteArrayBlockEncoding());
}
```
- [core/trino-spi/src/test/java/io/trino/spi/block/TestEncoderUtil.java:26] public -> final
- [core/trino-spi/src/test/java/io/trino/spi/block/TestEncoderUtil.java:33] please drop `public` on the test methods per https://trino.io/docs/current/develop/tests.html#conventions-and-recommendations
`Test classes should be defined as package-private and final.`

Diff (+2688/-909):
PR DESCRIPTION:
Last part of #26444 

OpenTelemetry (OTel) Extension re-write to support OTel Autoconfiguration.

**What do we get from it:**
- Support configuration according to [OTel Autoconfiguration](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-java/blob/main/sdk-extensions/autoconfigure/README.md)
- People using OTel standard configs will benefit from Quarkus configuration features.
- Access to features already implemented in the autoconfig of the SDK can be easily commanded.
- Make it easier to adopt future OTel features.
- Allow using the stock OTel exporters in the future.

**What was done:**
- Documentation to comply with the changes bellow and to improve some outdated aspects like the docker collector on Jaeger.
- Cleanup and refactoring. In special, move away from the main processor instrumentation related build steps.
- Simplify sampler and support its SPI hook
- Jakarta rebase with main
- Create substitutions for the Otel Auto-configuration code using reflection but not needed for runtime
- Remove obsolete config scanning steps
- Adapt IT tests, improve their resilience and speedup their execution
- Fix additional issues from dev mode reload
- Load missing properties, fix resources and sampler
- Create Otel SDK on a bean producer
- Autoconfigure wiring
- New build and runtime configurations based on OTel guidance, ensuring backwards compatibility.

**What was left for later:**
- Optimize Configuration relocations (might not even be needed because we

-This guide explains how your Quarkus application can utilize https://opentelemetry.io/[OpenTelemetry] to provide
+This guide explains how your Quarkus application can utilize https://opentelemetry.io/[OpenTelemetry] (OTel) to provide
 distributed tracing for interactive web applications.
 
+OpenTelemetry Metrics and Logging are not yet supported.
+
+[NOTE]
+====
+- Quarkus now supports the OpenTelemetry Autoconfiguration for Traces. The configurations match what you can see at
+https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-java/blob/main/sdk-extensions/autoconfigure/README.md[OpenTelemetry SDK Autoconfigure]
+with the `quarkus.*` prefix.
+
+- Extensions and the libraries they provide, are directly instrumented in Quarkus. The *use of the https://opentelemetry.io/docs/instrumentation/java/automatic/[OpenTelemetry Agent] is not needed nor recommended* due to context propagation issues between imperative and reactive libraries.
+====
+
 == Prerequisites

 === Create the configuration
 
-There are two ways to configure the default OTLP gRPC Exporter within the application.
-
-The first approach is by providing the properties within the `src/main/resources/application.properties` file:
+There are no mandatory configurations for the extension to work. If you need to change any of the default property values, here is an example on how to configure the default OTLP gRPC Exporter within the application, using the `src/main/resources/application.properties` file:
 
 [source,properties]
 ----
 quarkus.application.name=myservi

SUMMARY:
I would prefer that the config objects to use the @ConfigMapping approch. I know there were some initial bugs, but I think all of these are now fixed.

Let's not make a blocker for it. I can do that change later.

SUMMARY:
I'm not a big fan of cleaning up mess like this, but I guess it is easier this way.

My recommendation is for tests that are not QuarkusTests to just use `SmallRyeConfigBuilder` and `SmallRyeConfig` directly instead of `CofigProvider.getConfig`.

- [extensions/config-yaml/runtime/src/test/java/io/quarkus/config/yaml/runtime/ApplicationYamlTest.java:27] This can be simplified. If we are getting a `SmallRyeConfig` instance from the builder and testing against it, we don't need to register it into the factory, because this is not even a Quarkus test.
- [extensions/resteasy-classic/rest-client-config/runtime/src/test/java/io/quarkus/restclient/config/RestClientConfigTest.java:16] We can simplify this test by using `SmallRyeConfigBuilder` and `SmallRyeConfig`, and we don't need to set / release stuff, because this is not a Quarkus test.
- [test-framework/junit5-internal/src/main/java/io/quarkus/test/ConfigUtil.java:5] Not another `ConfigUtil`... we already have many of these (or similar). I know this will be for test only, and this method shouldn't go the main one, but I already get frustrated when I search for it to get 3 or 4 alternatives and always pick the wrong one :)

Maybe this could go into `io.quarkus.test.junit.launcher.ConfigUtil`?

SUMMARY:
Thanks @jjennnn, great start. I left a few comments on the implementation.

Also, it would be great if you could document your "exploration" of _what_ is considered unsafe, which for now it seems to be only some specific functions. If you could add that on the Jira issue for future reference that would be great.

POINTS:
- [hibernate-core/src/main/java/org/hibernate/query/sqm/function/SqmFunctionRegistry.java:114] How about, instead of validating by checking the function name, we avoid registering the "unsafe" functions in the first place, when safe mode is enabled? This way, when `findFunctionDescriptor` would return `null`, we know we are trying to call either an unknown or an unsafe function, and can throw when safe mode is enabled.

The check could leave in this class, as this registry is shared between the HQL and the Criteria paths, but also could be done at the call-sites (like you did in `SemanticQueryBuilder`, though I would move it to the common `getFunctionTemplate()` method path, and the parallel for criteria would be `SqmCriteriaNodeBuilder.getFunctionTemplate()`).
- [hibernate-core/src/test/java/org/hibernate/orm/test/query/sqm/HqlSafeModeTest.java:163] We should also add a test that uses `HibernateCriteriaBuilder.sql()` function directly, and not through the `function()` meta-function.
- [hibernate-core/src/test/java/org/hibernate/orm/test/query/sqm/SafeModeDebugTest.java:28] Slightly better name:

```suggestion
public class HqlSafeModeTest {
```

SUMMARY:
Thanks @jjennnn for the updates, second round of review.

POINTS:
- [hibernate-core/src/main/java/org/hibernate/boot/spi/SessionFactoryOptions.java:65] The Javadoc should not reference AI assistants, the safe mode is a generic feature of the Hibernate query interpreter. 

Also, this is a nitpick, but usually when adding new methods to interfaces I'd prefer putting them at the bottom, or if possible in this case grouping them with similar settings.
- [hibernate-core/src/main/java/org/hibernate/query/sqm/function/SqmFunctionRegistry.java:117] These checks are redundant, we can consolidate on a single error message.
- [hibernate-core/src/main/java/org/hibernate/query/sqm/function/SqmFunctionRegistry.java:55] I would rather not store mutable configuration in the registry itself. We could add the flag to `FunctionContributions` instead, so that it's available to both `Dialect` and any user-provided `FunctionContributor`.
- [hibernate-core/src/test/java/org/hibernate/orm/test/query/sqm/HqlSafeModeTest.java:60] Please also test `select function('column', e.name)`
- [hibernate-core/src/test/java/org/hibernate/orm/test/query/sqm/HqlSafeModeTest.java:82] Please also test `function('sql', 'SELECT 1')`
- [hibernate-core/src/test/java/org/hibernate/orm/test/query/sqm/HqlSafeModeTest.java:157] Please also test `criteriaBuilder.function("sql", "SELECT 1")`
- [hibernate-core/src/main/java/org/hibernate/query/sqm/internal/SqmCriteriaNodeBuilder.java:2451] This is a change in semantics that will break non-safe-mode use cases every time we try to retrieve an unknown function. Please revert this and move the safe-mode specific validation to `findFunctionDescriptor` instead.
- [hibernate-core/src/main/java/org/hibernate/query/hql/internal/SemanticQueryBuilder.java:4820] This is a change in semantics that will break non-safe-mode use cases every time we try to retrieve an unknown function. Please revert this and move the safe-mode specific validation to `findFunctionDescriptor` instead.

SUMMARY:
Thanks @Happy2110, the `normalized()` change will impact all queries regardless of which dialect is used so please roll that back.

Have you tested your changes locally against a SQL Server version < 2016?

POINTS:
- [hibernate-core/src/main/java/org/hibernate/query/common/TemporalUnit.java:344] This will break all compatibility between these units and the more granular ones, causing errors for e.g. duration arithmetic that previously worked as expected. Please rollback this change.
- [hibernate-core/src/test/java/org/hibernate/orm/test/dialect/function/SQLServerDateDiffTest.java:36] This test is very synthetic, and doesn't help with verifying the actual functionality. I would rather have a test with `@RequiresDialect(SQLServerDialect.class)` + actual queries that trigger the different datediff patterns (though I'm pretty sure we have plenty of tests that verify datetime arithmetic already).

POINTS:
- [hibernate-core/src/test/java/org/hibernate/orm/test/mapping/embeddable/EmbeddableInheritanceReplaceAfterPersistTest.java:26] Small nitpick: we already had the `org.hibernate.orm.test.inheritance.EmbeddableInheritanceReplaceTest` class, we could move this test to a dedicated method in there.
- [hibernate-core/src/main/java/org/hibernate/type/ComponentType.java:584] Actually, instead of changing the `replace` logic, I think we can just store the "correct" discriminator type, which includes the conversion information from `Class` to `String`: at line `114`, instead of `discriminator.getType()` we could store the `component.getDiscriminatorType()` instead. 

With that, I believe the existing `replace` logic should *almost* work as-is - we'll still need the `|| mappingType.isPolymorphic()` here to pick the correct instantiator.

SUMMARY:
Thanks for the contribution @Meehdi, I left a few comments. 

As with any primarily performance-related changes, we usually run (micro)benchmarks and profiling to verify we are actually seeing improvements in real-world scenarios, see for example [this repository](https://github.com/hibernate/hibernate-orm-benchmark).

Have you tried verifying if there is a significant gain (or loss) in performance with this change?

- [hibernate-core/src/main/java/org/hibernate/persister/entity/AbstractEntityPersister.java:2508] See previous comment
- [hibernate-core/src/test/java/org/hibernate/persister/entity/AbstractEntityPersisterTest.java:28] We already have existing tests which should check the behavior of the method you changed, no need to create one which artificially tests it through internal-only calls. It would be great if you could modify an existing one instead, adding any assertions if needed.

POINTS:
- [hibernate-core/src/main/java/org/hibernate/tool/schema/extract/spi/NameSpaceTablesInformation.java:25] Much better, let's apply Hibernate's [code style](https://hibernate.org/community/contribute/intellij-idea/#code-style):

```suggestion
		tables.put( identifierHelper.toMetaDataObjectName( tableInformation.getName().getTableName() ), tableInformation );
```
- [hibernate-core/src/test/java/org/hibernate/tool/schema/extract/spi/NameSpaceTablesInformationTest.java:41] This test is very "synthetic", and instantiating Hibernate internal classes is not portable as they might change in future versions. A good test reproduces a "real-world" scenario, the one where you experienced this particular bug, ensuring that no regressions are introduced. Could you try to isolate that in a simple reproducer? Thanks.

SUMMARY:
Thanks for the contribution! This is a great first pass, left a few comments.

POINTS:
- [hibernate-core/src/main/java/org/hibernate/loader/ast/internal/SingleIdLoadPlan.java:58] Storing the whole SQL AST tree here means having to keep a complex structure around for every single-id load plan, which means for every mapped entity type. It would be best to avoid this, especially seems the `SubselectFetch#createRegistrationHandler` method apparently doesn't use it anymore apart from a specific case.
- [hibernate-core/src/main/java/org/hibernate/loader/ast/internal/SingleIdLoadPlan.java:140] It would be great to avoid having to pass the `sqlAst` here. If you look at the `SubselectFetch.createRegistrationHandler` implementation, you can see it's used for 2 things:
1. checking whether the query has at least 1 root
2. passing the first `TableGroup` to the `StandardRegistrationHandler` constructor

Regarding point 2., the constructor parameter is never actually used - so we can deprecate this constructor and create one without the `TableGroup` parameter.

As for point 1., in `SingleIdLoadPlan` we always know the generated query has at least one root (we can be extra sure by adding an `assert !sqlAst.getQuerySpec().getFromClause().getRoots().isEmpty()` in the constructor) - so we don't really need to pass it for this check. I suggest creating a third signature of the `createRegistrationHandler` with the same parameters but no `TableGroup`, and deprecate the one with it.
- [hibernate-core/src/test/java/org/hibernate/orm/test/fetch/GenerateSubselectsOnJoinedAssociationTest.java:40] Small note, but if you want you can use `@DomainModel` instead of `@Jpa`, together with the `@SessionFactory` annotation they will allow your test methods to accept a `SessionFactoryScope` so you won't need to call `unwrap`.
- [hibernate-core/src/test/java/org/hibernate/orm/test/fetch/GenerateSubselectsOnJoinedAssociationTest.java:93] It would be nice to also test if the registered subselect fetches actually work when trying to initialize the `children` collections, and make some assertions on their contents as well.
